texto: This is your heritage,' he said, as if from this dance we could know about his own childhood, about the flavor and grit of tenement buildings in Spanish Harlem, and projects in Red Hook, and dance halls, and city parks, and about his own Paps, how he beat him, how he taught him to dance, as if we could hear Spanish in his movements, as if Puerto Rico was a man in a bathrobe, grabbing another beer from the fridge and raising it to drink, his head back, still dancing, still steeping and snapping perfectly in time.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Justin Torres: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “This is your heritage,' he said, as if from this dance we could know about his own childhood, abo…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, identity, puerto-rico pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de puerto-rico.

texto: All that pent up longing,” he says, “all that desire to flail and flap around. Them wings have needs.

texto: We hit and we kept on hitting; we were allowed to be what we were, frightened and vengeful — little animals, clawing at what we needed.
explicacao: Citação de Justin Torres sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “We hit and we kept on hitting; we were allowed to be what we were, frightened and vengeful — litt…” articula familia, animals, child em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como child e children.
